title: FINAL was scheduled for 2013 What happened
display_title: FINAL was scheduled for 2013 What happened
wikitext:
[]: FINAL was scheduled for 2013. What happened?             Quote Post by Dima » Sat Jan 17, 2015 7:29 am I suppose all of you remember that FINAL got a 2013 release date in New Year 2012 (along with the release date of Q). Khara was so sure about it so they thought ''why not announce a release date''? After all they had 2 years ahead of them in order to develop it. At the end of 2012 Khara removed the 2013 date from the official site, meaning that the film will be delayed and since then they haven't announced a new release date. At the 2014 Tokyo Film Festival Anno joked? that the final film may be released 4-6 years after Evangelion 3.0. We are now in 2015 and no release on sight so a 2016 seems very possible right now, at best. At first i was thinking 2015 as the most probable date but now nothing will surprise me. Not even a 2018 release date. Anyway my question is this. Why do you think Khara delayed the film since in 2012 they were so much confident for a 2013 release date?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Sat Jan 17, 2015 7:37 am ^ I guess Anno didn't think that working on Eva would take such a mental toll on him once again. After Q he seems to have been kinda down and he said, that he needed a rest and will get back to work on Shin after the Anima(tor) project. So considering, that the script is more or less finished (as he stated in an interview at the end of 2013) and doesn't intent to completely rewrite it, I guess late 2016 might be realistic to assume for a release of Shin. Also, the longer the gaps between the movies, the longer Eva seems to stay relevant. Thus they can stretch the merchandising and can make even more money.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 17, 2015 7:38 am Khara is a young studio. 4 Eva movies (which have the intercut production of an action film) right off the bat is ambitious for a baby studio, even if they weren't going to have the changes made in them that they did. A lot of it was simply miscalculation. The other part was Khara needing to get established in the business. Doing inbetweens for Ghibli and spear heading this Animator Expo is a great way to do that, and is needed by the studio at this point in its development, but it all takes time away from Eva Shin.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sat Jan 17, 2015 7:43 am robersora wrote: Also, the longer the gaps between the movies, the longer Eva seems to stay relevant. Thus they can stretch the merchandising and can make even more money.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Sat Jan 17, 2015 7:49 am ^ Yeah, but thankfully Anno hasn't lost that much artistic integrity for the sake of a few Million Yen just yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 17, 2015 9:43 am I remember when the whole Rebuild was slated to wrap up by 2009.

[]: Re: FINAL was scheduled for 2013. What happened?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jan 17, 2015 11:38 am Dima wrote: Why do you think Khara delayed the film since in 2012 they were so much confident for a 2013 release date?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jan 17, 2015 3:40 pm It's like an old fable you can tell your children: Once upon a time in 2006 In a Land where Marvel's Cinematic Universe was new & Instagram, Tumblr & Lorde did not yet exist A promise was made That Rebuild would be complete by late 2008/early 2009 And there was much rejoicing

Article: FINAL was scheduled for 2013 What happened
 young studio. 4 Eva movies (which have the intercut production of an action film) right off the bat is ambitious for a baby studio, even if they weren't going to have the changes made in them that they did. A lot of it was simply miscalculation. The other part was Khara needing to get established in the business. Doing inbetweens for Ghibli and spear heading this Animator Expo is a great way to do that, and is needed by the studio at this point in its development, but it all takes time away from Eva Shin.
